blocks.body: Passive Gerund — Passive gerund forms: being done, having been done.

{ "title": "8. Short summary", "items": [ "Simple passive gerund: being + V3 (being done, being written).", "Perfect passive gerund: having been + V3 (having been done).", "being done — for simultaneous actions or general statements.", "having been done — for actions that happened earlier (cause → effect).", "Used after verbs of feelings: like, hate, enjoy, avoid, mind.", "It is used after prepositions: without, after, before, despite, on.", "In stable expressions: be worth, be used to, look forward to, be afraid of.", "The perfect gerund is typical for academic and formal style." ] }
